skill.name: gmail-automation
skill.description: Automate Gmail tasks through Rube MCP, including sending messages, replying to threads, searching mail, managing labels, drafts, and attachments.
skill.summary: Use Rube MCP to automate common Gmail workflows with current tool schemas and explicit authorization.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution and weak-crypto findings are false positives caused by markdown inline code, tool names, and Gmail ID text in SKILL.md. The real risk is medium because the skill instructs agents to use an external Rube MCP endpoint for authorized Gmail actions that can read, send, label, draft, and download email content.

medium_findings.title: External MCP Handles Sensitive Gmail Actions
medium_findings.description: The skill directs users to add https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server and then use Gmail tools for sending email, replying to threads, searching messages, labels, drafts, and attachments. This appears legitimate, but it creates privacy and account-action risk because authorized email content and send operations pass through an external service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external endpoint and Gmail tool workflows are explicit in SKILL.md. The risk is about sensitive authorized email operations, not confirmed malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The numerous Ruby or shell backtick findings are inline code spans, fenced examples, parameter names, and tool names in documentation. I found no executable script, Ruby code, shell command, or user-input command construction in the only scanned file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged patterns are visible as markdown formatting and documentation examples. The repository file list contains only SKILL.md and no runnable helper scripts.

low_findings.title: Weak-Crypto and Reconnaissance Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptographic-algorithm and reconnaissance detections do not correspond to cryptographic code, host discovery, or network scanning. The relevant lines describe the Gmail skill metadata, Gmail response IDs, contact lookup steps, query syntax, and quick reference entries.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation text for Gmail workflows. No code implements cryptography, system reconnaissance, or network reconnaissance.

content.value_statement: Managing Gmail by hand is slow when messages, labels, drafts, and attachments repeat across workflows. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Rube MCP Gmail actions.
actual_capabilities: Guides setup for connecting Rube MCP and authorizing Gmail access.
Explains how to send new Gmail messages with recipients, subject, body, carbon copy, blind carbon copy, and attachments.
Documents reply workflows that keep messages inside an existing Gmail thread.
Shows how to search Gmail with query syntax, pagination, message IDs, and payload options.
Covers label creation, label updates, bulk message labeling, archive actions, and read-state changes.
Describes draft creation, draft updates, draft sending, and attachment handling through Gmail tools.
limitations: Requires a working Rube MCP server and an active Gmail OAuth connection.
Does not provide its own Gmail API implementation or standalone scripts.
Gmail rate limits, message size limits, and attachment expiration still apply.
Sending drafts or messages should require explicit user approval before execution.

use_cases.title: Process Support Mailboxes
use_cases.description: Search incoming support mail, apply labels, archive resolved messages, and prepare replies from the same workflow.

use_cases.title: Manage Sales Follow-Ups
use_cases.description: Find relevant threads, draft replies, keep subject threading intact, and send approved follow-up messages.

use_cases.title: Organize Personal Inbox Tasks
use_cases.description: Create labels, mark messages read or unread, search attachments, and draft routine responses.

prompt_templates.title: Search My Inbox
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Gmail for unread messages from finance@example.com from the last 30 days. Show sender, subject, date, and a short summary.

prompt_templates.title: Draft A Reply
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the latest thread from Jordan about the contract review. Draft a concise reply that confirms receipt and asks for the missing attachment.

prompt_templates.title: Organize Matching Messages
prompt_templates.prompt: Create or find a label named Clients/Invoices. Apply it to invoice emails with attachments from this quarter, then report how many messages changed.

prompt_templates.title: Run A Controlled Mail Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for starred customer emails older than seven days, summarize each thread, draft follow-ups, and wait for my approval before sending anything.

output_examples.input: Find unread invoice emails with attachments from January and label them Finance/Invoices.
output_examples.output: The assistant lists matching messages, confirms or creates the label, applies it to selected messages, and reports the final count.

output_examples.input: Reply to the latest email from Priya about tomorrow's meeting.
output_examples.output: The assistant finds the thread, drafts a reply in the same conversation, and asks for approval before sending.

output_examples.input: Prepare a draft to send the project update to the team.
output_examples.output: The assistant creates a Gmail draft with recipients, subject, and body, then returns the draft status for review.

best_practices: Search Rube tools first so parameter names match the current Gmail tool schema.
Resolve label IDs, message IDs, thread IDs, and contact emails before modifying or sending messages.
Require explicit user approval before sending emails, sending drafts, deleting labels, or making bulk changes.
anti_patterns: Do not run broad payload searches when metadata is enough.
Do not use Gmail web UI IDs, UUIDs, or display label names where tool calls require internal IDs.
Do not send or modify bulk messages without previewing the target set first.

faq.question: Does this skill connect directly to Gmail?
faq.answer: No. It guides Gmail actions through Rube MCP, which must be configured and authorized separately.

faq.question: Can it send email automatically?
faq.answer: It documents send and draft-send workflows, but users should approve every outgoing message before execution.

faq.question: Can it manage Gmail labels?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains listing labels, creating labels, updating labels, deleting user labels, and applying labels to messages.

faq.question: Can it work with attachments?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers downloading attachments and using attachment objects when sending messages or drafts.

faq.question: What should I verify before bulk changes?
faq.answer: Verify the search query, message count, target IDs, and requested label changes before running a batch operation.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill is documentation-only, but it enables sensitive Gmail reads and sends through an external MCP service.
